GlobalPolicy.Redaction: Never log secrets, credentials, raw tokens, or high-risk payloads beyond bounded existing policies.
GlobalPolicy.FreshEvidence: Completion claims require fresh command output or documented blockers.

VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.Expected: Pinned base package specifier for server and TUI loading, legacy TUI subpath migration, vv-controller default agent, managed agents/skills, tools.apply_patch false, role-reference model fields, and seeded vvoc schema v3.

VF-CONFIG-SYNC.Expected: Canonical vvoc schema v3 and managed runtime/TUI registrations are refreshed while role assignments, plugin toggles, unrelated TUI settings, and tuple options survive; invalid current configs fail before rewrite.

VF-STRICT-CURRENT-CUTOVER.Expected: Old configs, SDK paths, and hash algorithms fail loudly or are ignored rather than repaired or used as compatibility fallbacks.

VF-LAYERED-CONFIG.Expected: vvoc, runtime, and TUI reads choose env, nearest project, global, then defaults where allowed; project writes stay inside selected project root.

VF-VVOC-LAUNCH.Expected: Launch plan sets OPENCODE_CONFIG and VVOC_CONFIG, conditionally sets OPENCODE_TUI_CONFIG for an existing selected file, forwards args, preserves child exit code, and reports missing project runtime layer with install hint.

VF-CONTEXT-INSPECTION.Expected: /context registers only when enabled, requires an active session, keeps latest provider usage as measured truth, derives percentages only from a positive context limit, reconciles observable current schemas with active post-compaction per-tool history, marks connected MCP schema catalogs unavailable when public OpenCode APIs omit them, attributes MCP tools by unique longest sanitized prefix, fails closed on collisions, renders scrollable Overview/Tools/MCP tabs, and handles missing state without crashing.

VF-RELEASE-AUTOMATION.Expected: OpenTUI Solid transforms preload before all Bun test discovery; tui:local replaces only the managed package with the local dist export inside an isolated temporary config; release:bump pushes no tag and dispatches publish.yml with exact version/SHA inputs; CI verifies release consistency, typecheck, lint, format, tests, and pack before npm publication, then creates the matching remote tag and GitHub Release; reruns accept existing npm bytes only when gitHead matches the requested SHA.

VF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.Expected: Built-in roles are visible, custom roles round-trip, concrete assignments normalize, and built-in role deletion is blocked.

VF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.Expected: Supported vv-role:* fields resolve to concrete model selections and unknown roles fail without fallback.

VF-PRESET-SWITCH.Expected: Preset validates all roles and orchestration before one atomic write; custom presets without orchestration preserve the current profile; invalid input leaves original bytes unchanged.

VF-HASHLINE-EDIT.Expected: Read output emits current anchors, anchored edits preserve text envelope, and stale or old hash anchors are rejected.

VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.Expected: Guardian resolves config/model, logs bounded markers, auto-allows only below threshold, and falls back to manual approval on higher risk or failure.

VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.Expected: vv-controller receives universal guidance plus exactly one startup-resolved profile policy; other primaries receive universal guidance only; known subagents are excluded.

VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.Expected: Provider-bound payloads are redacted and local completions/tool execution restore placeholders where needed.

VF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.Expected: Work-item tools create deterministic IDs, enforce explicit mode/reviewers, list lifecycle and review-round metadata including bounded recovery excerpts, and close only ready items.

VF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.Expected: Tracked launches require VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID and valid state; invalid launches are rejected and untracked launches pass through.

VF-RESULT-PARSING.Expected: Tracked outputs parse strict top blocks with separated bodies, diagnose missing blank lines before body text, update explicit round state with bounded excerpts, repair only bounded resumable envelopes without changing outcomes, and halt on malformed or hard-stop results while preserving controller-visible result context.

VF-LOOP-GATE.Expected: Rounds one and two are allowed and round three is blocked deterministically without semantic heuristics.

VF-WORKFLOW-PERSISTENCE.Expected: Per-session workflow state hydrates, snapshots, switches, preserves optional bounded excerpt fields, and cleans up without throwing on missing or corrupt files.

VF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.Expected: Known plugins can be enabled or disabled, sync preserves values, list reports status, and disabled plugins return empty hooks.

VF-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILE.Expected: Effective startup config resolves one profile; vv-controller receives only its concrete policy; old v3 config defaults to balanced; preset writes are atomic; invalid explicit values fail without fallback.

V-M-CONFIG-LAYERS.Expected: Global/project/effective runtime and TUI sources honor env precedence, json/jsonc selection, and project-local write boundaries.

V-M-CLI-CONFIG.Expected: Dedicated tui.json(c) mutation writes the pinned base package, migrates legacy subpath specs, preserves comments, unrelated entries, and tuple options; host compatibility, install/sync idempotence, parse state, and registration state are deterministic.

Checks.Command: bun test scripts/tui-local.test.ts scripts/release-bump.test.ts scripts/release-check.test.ts scripts/release-summary.test.ts

V-M-RELEASE-AUTOMATION.Expected: The Bun test runner preloads @opentui/solid/preload before test module discovery; local release preparation pushes only the branch and dispatches exact version/SHA inputs; publish.yml is explicit-dispatch-only, orders tests before npm publication and npm publication before remote tag creation, and configures a repository-local GitHub Actions bot identity before creating an annotated tag.

V-M-PLUGIN-CONTEXT-TUI.Expected: Measured provider usage, positive-limit and unavailable percentages, compaction cutoff, schema/history reconciliation, skill non-duplication, unavailable connected MCP schema catalogs, known-zero disconnected MCP schemas, longest-prefix collisions, stable sorting, command gating, bounded failures, host-owned tab composition, middle-half host geometry, preloaded reactive keyboard navigation and cleanup, scrolling, narrow rendering, and post-replacement xlarge sizing remain deterministic.

V-M-WORKFLOW-PROTOCOL.Expected: Strict top-block parsing preserves separated body text and reports missing blank lines before body text with actionable diagnostics.

V-M-WORKFLOW-REPAIR.Expected: Repair prompts stay format-only and explicitly move body text below the required blank line while preserving work item, status, route, and outcome.

V-M-WORKFLOW-STATE.Expected: Work-item state stores deterministic bounded excerpts on implementer hard stops and reviewer results without changing lifecycle semantics.

V-M-WORKFLOW-TOOLING.Expected: work_item_list exposes implementer and reviewer recovery excerpts in a stable JSON-friendly shape.

V-M-PLUGIN-WORKFLOW.Expected: Protocol errors, state-application errors, and blocked or needs_context hard stops include bounded result excerpts while preserving fail-closed behavior.

V-M-WORKFLOW-PERSISTENCE.Expected: Optional bounded excerpt fields round-trip through snapshot/hydrate and corrupt excerpt shapes are rejected with the existing safe-null behavior.

Gate-Phase-11.Goal: Documentation, verification artifacts, build, pack, and release checks are current.

Gate-Phase-12.Goal: Plugin runtime toggle behavior is covered.

Gate-Phase-13.Goal: Workflow persistence works across process restarts and session switches.

Gate-GRACE-4-MIGRATION.Goal: GRACE 4 .grace state is valid after migration.
